texts.full_text: a loss of the cranial implant. Brommeland et al. \[[@pone.0172694.ref004]\] for example, showed that out of 87 patients, 37 (36%) suffered from complications, whereby even 22 lost their primary implant. The most common causes were surgical site infections, affecting eight patients (9.2%) and bone flap resorption in fourteen patients (19.7%).
